extensionDescription.message: AI-powered instant speech translation for all video meetings. Break language barriers with real-time voice translation.

clickToStart.message: Click "Open Sokuji" below to start real-time translation.

quickStartInstructions.message: Configure your API key (OpenAI or Gemini) → Select "Sokuji_Virtual_Mic" as microphone → Start speaking!

gatherTownGuidance.message: To use Sokuji, please select "Sokuji Virtual Microphone" in your microphone settings.

wherebyGuidance.message: To use Sokuji, please select "Sokuji Virtual Microphone" in your microphone settings and disable Noise reduction for better performance.

defaultGuidance.message: To use Sokuji, please select "Sokuji Virtual Microphone" in your microphone settings.

discordGuidance.message: To use Sokuji, please select "Sokuji Virtual Microphone" in your microphone settings and disable Noise Suppression in Discord's Voice settings for better performance.

slackGuidance.message: To use Sokuji in Slack Huddles, please start a Huddle, then select "Sokuji Virtual Microphone" in your Video and Mic settings, and disable Noise Suppression in Audio & Video preferences for better performance.

teamsGuidance.message:
If you don't see the complete audio device list in Teams settings:
1) Temporarily disable the Sokuji extension and refresh the page
2) Allow Teams to access your microphone and camera when prompted
3) Re-enable Sokuji extension and refresh the page again
4) Allow Sokuji to access your microphone when prompted.
Now you should see both real and "Sokuji Virtual Microphone" devices in your settings.

jitsiGuidance.message: To use Sokuji in Jitsi Meet, open Settings > Devices and select "Sokuji Virtual Microphone" as your microphone. Then open Advanced audio settings and turn off Noise suppression, Echo cancellation, and Automatic gain control for clear translated audio.

desktopCtaBody.message: A system-wide virtual mic for OBS, YouTube, Twitch and any desktop app. Windows · macOS · Linux.

browserSitesHeading.message: Prefer the browser? Sokuji works on these — click to open:

detectFailBody.message: Refresh the page and reopen Sokuji.
